questions.text: A particle moves from position r= 3i+2j-6k to r= 14i+13j+9k under the action of a Force F= 8i+2j+6k. Find the work done by the force:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.
This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.
This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.
This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.

According to the calculation given below, this is the correct answer. Total Work Done= work done in x + work done in y + work done in z Since, Work done= Force x distance moved in the direction of force Distance moved from r1 to r2 is= r2 – r1 = (14i+13j+9k) - (3i+2j-6k) = (11i+11j+15k)Force= 8i+2j+6kHence, Work done (x)= force (x) x Distance moved in x-axis = 8 x 11 = 88 Work done (y)= force(y) x Distance moved in y-axis = 2 x 11 = 22 Work done (z)= force (y) x Distance moved in z-axis = 6 x 15 = 90Total work done= 88 + 22 + 90=200

questions.explanation: Work done = force×displacement Given data:F=8i+2j+6k N Now displacement, d=(14i+13j+9k) — (3i+2j-6k)d=(14–3)i+(13–2)j+(9-[-6])k d=11i+11j+15k Now, W=F×d W= (8i+2j+6k) × (11i+11j+15k) W=(11×8)+(2×11)+(6×15)W=88+22+90 W=200J

questions.text: A vector such as the velocity of a body undergoing uniform translational motion, which can be displaced parallel to itself and applied to any point is known as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A: A vector that has a magnitude of 1 is a unit vector. It is also known as Direction Vector. (Hence incorrect)

A vector is a quantity that has both magnitude, as well as direction. Free vectors refers to a vector which is neither a point nor a line, and something that can move freely around the space though it has a fixed magnitude and fixed direction and can be displaced parallel to itself. (Hence correct option)
Option C: The null vector is defined to have zero magnitude and no particular direction. (Hence incorrect)

Option D: A position vector is defined as a vector that symbolizes either the position or the location of any given point with respect to any arbitrary reference point like the origin. The direction of the position vector always points from the origin of that vector towards a given point.
Option E: A resultant vector is defined as a single vector that produces the same effect as is produced by a number of vectors collectively.

questions.explanation: A free vector is a vector that can be displaced parallel to itself.

questions.text: All of the following is/are scalar quantities except:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Temperature: Temperature is a scalar quantity. It is described by a numerical value (magnitude) that represents the degree of hotness or coldness of an object or a system. It does not have a specific direction associated with it.
Density: Density is a scalar quantity. It represents the mass per unit volume of a substance. Density is described solely by its numerical value and does not have a direction.
Volume: Volume is a scalar quantity. It represents the amount of space occupied by an object or a substance. It is described only by its magnitude and does not have a direction.

Scalar Quantities are defined as the physical quantities that have magnitude or size only. For example, distance, speed, mass, density, etc.However, vector quantities are those physical quantities that have both magnitude and direction like displacement, velocity, acceleration, force, etc.

Speed: Speed is a scalar quantity. It represents the rate at which an object covers a certain distance. It is described solely by its numerical value (magnitude) and does not have a specific direction associated with it.

questions.explanation: Scalar quantities are those that only have magnitude, unlike vector quantities that have direction as well as magnitude.Temperature, density, volume, and speed all have magnitudes only. Force, however, has magnitude as well as direction. Therefore, it is a vector.

questions.text: What is the projection of E = 2i-3j+6k onto the direction of vector F=i+2j+2k?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: As per calculations, only 8/3 is the right option.

To find the projection of vector E onto the direction ofvector F, we can use the formula: Projection of E onto F = (E dot F) / |F| First, let's calculate the dot product of E and F: E dot F =(2)(1) + (-3)(2) + (6)(2) = 2 - 6 + 12 = 8 Next, we need to find the magnitude of vector F: |F| = √(1² + 2² + 2²) = √9 = 3 Now we can calculate the projection: Projection of E onto F= (E dot F) / |F| = 8 / 3 ≈ 2.67 Therefore, the projection of vector E onto the direction ofvector F is approximately 2.67.
As per calculations, only 8/3 is the right option.

As per calculations, only 8/3 is the right option.

questions.text: Instantaneous velocity is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.
This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.

Instantaneous velocity is the velocity of an object at a specific instant in time. It is the rate of change of the object's displacement with respect to time at that precise moment. In other words, it is the slope of the object's position-time graph at a particular time point.Mathematically, the instantaneous velocity (v) of an object at time t can be expressed as the derivative of its displacement (s) with respect to time (t):v(t) = ∆s/∆twhere:v(t) = Instantaneous velocity at time t∆s = Infinitesimal change in displacement (distance traveled) during an infinitesimally short time intervaldt = Infinitesimal change in time during that same infinitesimally short time intervalIf we want to know the instantaneous velocity at a specific moment t₀, we take the limit of the average velocity over a shorter and shorter time interval Δt approaching zero:If we want to know the instantaneous velocity at a specific moment t₀, we take the limit of the average velocity over a shorter and shorter time interval Δt approaching zero:v(t₀) = lim(Δt → 0) Δs / ΔtAs the time interval Δt becomes infinitesimally small (Δt → 0), we get the instantaneous velocity at time t₀.In summary, instantaneous velocity gives us the exact velocity of an object at an exact point in time, taking into account any changes in speed and direction that may be occurring continuously during motion. It is a fundamental concept in calculus and physics, as it allows us to study the behavior of objects with precision as time changes.
This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.

questions.explanation: Instantaneous velocity is the velocity of an object at a specific instant in time. It is the rate of change of the object's displacement with respect to time at that precise moment. In other words, it is the slope of the object's position-time graph at a particular time point.Mathematically, the instantaneous velocity (v) of an object at time t can be expressed as the derivative of its displacement (s) with respect to time (t):v(t) = ∆s/∆twhere:v(t) = Instantaneous velocity at time t∆s = Infinitesimal change in displacement (distance traveled) during an infinitesimally short time intervaldt = Infinitesimal change in time during that same infinitesimally short time intervalIf we want to know the instantaneous velocity at a specific moment t₀, we take the limit of the average velocity over a shorter and shorter time interval Δt approaching zero:If we want to know the instantaneous velocity at a specific moment t₀, we take the limit of the average velocity over a shorter and shorter time interval Δt approaching zero:v(t₀) = lim(Δt → 0) Δs / ΔtAs the time interval Δt becomes infinitesimally small (Δt → 0), we get the instantaneous velocity at time t₀.In summary, instantaneous velocity gives us the exact velocity of an object at an exact point in time, taking into account any changes in speed and direction that may be occurring continuously during motion. It is a fundamental concept in calculus and physics, as it allows us to study the behavior of objects with precision as time changes.

questions.explanation: The direction of the angular velocity is along the axis of rotation. For an object rotating clockwise, the angular velocity points away from you along the axis of rotation. For an object rotating counterclockwise, the angular velocity points toward you along the axis of rotation.

questions.text: Consider a car is travelling for one hour. Which of the following trips have the same average velocity?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Car travels 20 km due east and car travels 70 km due east

options.text: Car travels 40 km due east, then turns around and travels 20 km due west and a car that travels 20 km due east
options.explanation: Average velocity = total displacement/total timeWe know that displacement is a vector quantity. Therefore, let's assume that the west direction is negative and the east direction is positive throughout this question.For option B:Average velocity of the 1st car ={+40 + (-20)}/1= 20 km/hrAverage velocity of the 2nd car = +20/1= 20 km/hrThus B is the correct option.As it is numerical so it can have only one possible answer.

Therefore, let's assume that the west direction is negative and the east direction is positive throughout this question.For option B:Average velocity of the 1st car = {+40 + (-20)}/1 = 20 km/hrAverage velocity of the 2nd car = +20/1 = 20 km/hrThus B is the correct option.

questions.text: Numbers are expressed in standard form called scientific notation, which employs powers of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Options A is incorrect because it does not represent the correct base used in scientific notation. Scientific notation uses powers of 10 to express numbers.
Options B is incorrect because it does not represent the correct base used in scientific notation. Scientific notation uses powers of 10 to express numbers.
A number is expressed in scientific notation when it is written as the product of a factor and a power of 10. Examples: Scientific Notation of 2,700,000,000 is 2.7 × 10⁹.

questions.text: If A + B= 7i^ +7k^ and A-B= -i^ + k^, then the magnitude of A will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option does not match the calculated magnitude of A, which is 5. Option A is incorrect.
This option does not match the calculated magnitude of A, which is 5. Option B is incorrect.

Given:A + B = 7i^ + 7k^A - B = -i^ + k^To find the magnitude of A, we need to eliminate B from these equations. We can do this by adding the two equations together:(A + B) + (A - B) = (7i^ + 7k^) + (-i^ + k^)Simplifying the left side and right side separately:2A = 6i^ + 8k^Dividing both sides by 2:A = 3i^ + 4k^Now we have the vector A. To find its magnitude, we can use the formula:|A| = √(Ax² + Ay² + Az²)In this case, Ax = 3, Ay = 0, and Az = 4. Substituting these values into the formula:|A| = √(3² + 0² + 4²)|A| = √(3² + 0^2 + 4^2)= √(9 + 0 + 16)= √25= 5Therefore, the magnitude of A is 5. Hence, option C is the correct answer.
This option does not match the calculated magnitude of A, which is 5. Option D is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Given:A + B = 7i^ + 7k^A - B = -i^ + k^To find the magnitude of A, we need to eliminate B from these equations. We can do this by adding the two equations together:(A + B) + (A - B) = (7i^ + 7k^) + (-i^ + k^)Simplifying the left side and right side separately:2A = 6i^ + 8k^Dividing both sides by 2:A = 3i^ + 4k^Now we have the vector A. To find its magnitude, we can use the formula:|A| = √(Ax² + Ay² + Az²)In this case, Ax = 3, Ay = 0, and Az = 4. Substituting these values into the formula:|A| = √(3² + 0² + 4²)= √(9 + 0 + 16)= √25= 5Therefore, the magnitude of A is 5. Hence, option C is the correct answer.

questions.text: If two forces F1 and F2 act on a body and are balanced by a frictional force F3 so that the body is in equilibrium, then which of the following diagrams correctly represents the sum of vectors?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In terms of a triangle, this would mean that the vectors representing F1 and F2 should form a closed triangle, where the resultant vector (sum of vectors) points in the opposite direction to F3.Hence, option a is incorrect.

In terms of a triangle, this would mean that the vectors representing F1 and F2 should form a closed triangle, where the resultant vector (sum of vectors) points in the opposite direction to F3.Hence, option b is incorrect.

If the forces F1 and F2 are balanced by the frictional force F3, it means that the vector sum of F1 and F2 should be equal in magnitude and opposite in direction to the vector F3.In terms of a triangle, this would mean that the vectors representing F1 and F2 should form a closed triangle, where the resultant vector (sum of vectors) points in the opposite direction to F3.Hence, option c is correct.

In terms of a triangle, this would mean that the vectors representing F1 and F2 should form a closed triangle, where the resultant vector (sum of vectors) points in the opposite direction to F3.Hence, option D is incorrect.
